name: Testing Package Examples
description: Create runnable examples for the @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils/testing package. IMPORTANT: After implementing each example, you MUST run it to verify it works: `npx tsx <example>.ts`. The testing package provides mock server infrastructure for testing Algorand clients (algod/indexer/kmd). NOTE: Examples should work whether or not mock servers are running - they should check availability and provide helpful messages.

userStories.title: Infrastructure Setup - Verify Shared Utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify shared utilities work and set up the testing examples folder structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify examples/shared/utils.ts exists and exports: printHeader(), printStep(), printInfo(), printSuccess(), printError(), formatBytes(), formatHex()
Create examples/testing folder if it doesn't exist
Verify the root examples/package.json exists
Verify the root examples/tsconfig.json has correct ESM module settings and path mapping for @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils/testing
npm install runs without errors in examples directory
TypeScript compiles without errors: npx tsc --noEmit
userStories.notes: No mock server required for infrastructure setup

userStories.title: Configuration Constants Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see all the configuration constants available in the testing package for reference, including default tokens, ports, and environment variable names.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/testing/01-configuration-constants.ts
Import and display DEFAULT_TOKEN constant (64 'a' characters)
Import and display MOCK_PORTS constant with all port configurations (algod: 8000, indexer: 8002, kmd: 8001)
Import and display EXTERNAL_URL_ENV_VARS mapping (algod -> MOCK_ALGOD_URL, indexer -> MOCK_INDEXER_URL, kmd -> MOCK_KMD_URL)
Document the ClientType union type values: 'algod' | 'indexer' | 'kmd'
Explain the purpose of each constant in the mock server infrastructure
Run the example with `npx tsx testing/01-configuration-constants.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No mock server required - just displays constants

userStories.title: Test Data Constants Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see all the test data constants available in the testing package that match pre-recorded mock server responses.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/testing/02-test-data-constants.ts
Import and display TEST_ADDRESS constant (Algorand address string)
Import and display TEST_APP_ID constant (718348254) and TEST_APP_ID_WITH_BOXES (742949200)
Import and display TEST_BOX_NAME constant (base64-encoded box name)
Import and display TEST_ASSET_ID constant (705457144)
Import and display TEST_TXID constant (transaction ID string)
Import and display TEST_ROUND constant (24099447)
Explain that these constants match pre-recorded HAR file responses in the mock server
Run the example with `npx tsx testing/02-test-data-constants.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No mock server required - just displays constants

userStories.title: Health Check Utility Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use checkServerHealth() to verify mock server availability before running tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/testing/03-health-check.ts
Import checkServerHealth, MOCK_PORTS, and EXTERNAL_URL_ENV_VARS from @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils/testing
Demonstrate checkServerHealth() with default timeout (5000ms)
Demonstrate checkServerHealth() with custom timeout parameter (e.g., 2000ms)
Show how to check health of algod mock server URL (from MOCK_ALGOD_URL or localhost:8000)
Show how to check health of indexer mock server URL (from MOCK_INDEXER_URL or localhost:8002)
Show how to check health of kmd mock server URL (from MOCK_KMD_URL or localhost:8001)
Handle the case where servers are not running gracefully with informative output
Display whether each server is reachable (true/false)
Run the example with `npx tsx testing/03-health-check.ts` and verify it completes successfully (should work even if servers are down)
userStories.notes: Example should work whether or not mock servers are running - it just reports status

userStories.title: Mock Server Connection Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use getMockServer() to connect to a mock server and understand the MockServer interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/testing/04-mock-server-connection.ts
Import getMockServer, checkServerHealth, EXTERNAL_URL_ENV_VARS, and MOCK_PORTS from @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils/testing
Document the MockServer interface properties: baseUrl (string) and clientType (ClientType)
First check server health before attempting connection
Demonstrate getMockServer('algod') to connect to algod mock server
Show how getMockServer reads from environment variables (MOCK_ALGOD_URL, etc.)
Handle the case where environment variable is not set with informative error message
Handle the case where server is not reachable with informative error message
Use try/catch to gracefully handle connection failures
If connection succeeds, display the MockServer baseUrl and clientType
Run the example with `npx tsx testing/04-mock-server-connection.ts` and verify it handles all cases appropriately
userStories.notes: Requires MOCK_ALGOD_URL environment variable and running mock server for full demo - otherwise shows helpful error messages

userStories.title: Vitest Global Setup Factory Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use createGlobalSetup() to create custom Vitest global setup functions for mock server lifecycle management.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/testing/05-global-setup-factory.ts
Import createGlobalSetup from @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils/testing
Document the function signature: createGlobalSetup(clientType: ClientType) returns an async setup function
Demonstrate creating a custom global setup for algod: createGlobalSetup('algod')
Demonstrate creating a custom global setup for indexer: createGlobalSetup('indexer')
Demonstrate creating a custom global setup for kmd: createGlobalSetup('kmd')
Explain that the returned function is async and returns a teardown function
Show example vitest.config.ts usage with globalSetup option
Explain that DEBUG_MOCK_SERVER=true enables verbose logging
Explain that the setup reads .env file from project root for configuration
Run the example with `npx tsx testing/05-global-setup-factory.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: This example documents the factory pattern without invoking the setup (no server needed)

userStories.title: Pre-built Global Setups Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see the pre-built global setup functions available for common client types and how to use them in Vitest configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/testing/06-prebuilt-global-setups.ts
Import algodGlobalSetup, indexerGlobalSetup, kmdGlobalSetup from @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils/testing
Show that these are pre-configured setup functions ready to use
Explain algodGlobalSetup connects to mock algod server
Explain indexerGlobalSetup connects to mock indexer server
Explain kmdGlobalSetup connects to mock kmd server
Show example vitest.config.ts for each global setup
Show example tests/globalSetup.ts that re-exports the setup
Explain the difference between using pre-built setups vs createGlobalSetup()
Run the example with `npx tsx testing/06-prebuilt-global-setups.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: This example documents usage patterns without invoking setups (no server needed)

userStories.title: Integration Example - Complete Test Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see a complete example showing how all testing package components work together for a real test scenario.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/testing/07-integration-example.ts
Import all major exports: getMockServer, checkServerHealth, DEFAULT_TOKEN, MOCK_PORTS, EXTERNAL_URL_ENV_VARS, TEST_ADDRESS, TEST_APP_ID, TEST_ROUND
First check if mock algod server is available using checkServerHealth()
If server is available, connect using getMockServer('algod')
Show how to construct a client configuration using MockServer.baseUrl and DEFAULT_TOKEN
Display mock server connection details
Show which test data constants can be used with the connected mock server
If server is unavailable, display helpful setup instructions
Reference the README.md mock server section for local development setup
Run the example with `npx tsx testing/07-integration-example.ts` and verify it handles both connected and disconnected states
userStories.notes: Works with or without mock server - provides helpful guidance in either case

userStories.title: Verification Script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script to run all testing package examples and verify they work.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/testing/verify-all.sh bash script
Script runs each example in order (01 through 07)
Script reports success/failure for each example
Script exits with non-zero code if any example fails
All 7 examples pass when running ./verify-all.sh (even without mock servers running)
Update root examples/verify-all.sh to include testing examples if not already
userStories.notes: All examples should be designed to pass regardless of mock server availability
